records.ety: [From cousin, hence, literally, to deceive through pretext of relationship, F. cousiner.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Cozened (-'nd); p. pr. & vb. n. Cozening (-'n-?ng). ]

defs: To cheat; to defraud; to beguile; to deceive, usually by small arts, or in a pitiful way.
quotes: He had cozened the world by fine phrases.
Children may be cozened into a knowledge of the letters.
Goring loved no man so well but that he would cozen him, and expose him to public mirth for having been cozened.

defs: To deceive; to cheat; to act deceitfully.
